sourceMovie.title: Pretty Baby
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pretty Baby works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Interview with the Vampire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pretty Baby clicked for you, Interview with the Vampire (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pretty Baby?
faq.answer: Try Interview with the Vampire, Perfume: The Story of a Murderer, The Whale—each shares DNA with Pretty Baby while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pretty Baby?
faq.answer: Pretty Baby (1978) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pretty Baby worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
